segments.s165-g591: ندانم is the negative present of دانستن 'I do not know' (classical style; the verb is fronted). به توران گراید is a clause meaning 'he turns/inclines to Turan' — گراید is a classical present/subjunctive form meaning 'may turn / will turn', with به marking the direction 'toward'. به مهر is an adverbial phrase 'in love / in affection' (به + مهر 'with affection'), modifying the verb گراید.
segments.s165-g592: وگر is a conditional/contrastive 'and if / but if' introducing the alternative. سوی ایران کند is parallel to the previous clause: سوی 'toward', ایران the object, کند the verb 'does/makes/turns' (classical usage for 'goes/turns'). پاک چهر is a two-word descriptive phrase 'pure-faced' or 'with a clean/pure face' (پاک = 'pure/clean', چهر = 'face/appearance') placed after the verb as a predicate/adverbial complement.
segments.s165-g569: پراندیشه شد is a compound predication: پراندیشه (an adjective 'full of thought / pensive') plus شد (past 3rd-person singular 'became'). The following جان افراسیاب is the subject expressed after the verb (typical in epic/literary inversion): 'the soul of Afrasiab became pensive.'
segments.s165-g570: چنین گفت is 'thus said' (چنین = 'thus/so' + گفت = 'said'). با introduces an instrumental/associative phrase ('with'); دیده کرده پرآب is a noun + participle + adjective cluster meaning 'eyes made wet' (دیده = 'eye', کرده = past participle 'made/done' modifying دیده, پرآب = 'full of water / wet' describing those eyes). The whole phrase means 'he said thus, with eyes made wet (i.e. teary-eyed).'
segments.s165-g571: که introduces a content clause 'that'. من گفته‌ام is perfect tense 'I have said / I have told' (گفته‌ام = past participle plus 1st-person auxiliary). پیش ازین (preposed here as separate parts) means 'before this' (پیش = before, از + ین = 'of/this' older orthography). داستان = 'tale/story'. Together: 'that I have told this tale before.'
segments.s165-g572: نبودی is a past negative copula/impersonal 'there was not / there was none.' بران is a fused form of بر آن 'on/with respect to that'; گفته is noun 'statement/said thing'; همداستان is an adjective 'like‑minded / in agreement.' The clause means 'there was none who agreed with that statement.'
segments.s165-g573: چنین گفت با من = 'thus said to me' (با + من marks the indirect object 'to me' in older/literary usage). یکی هوشمند = 'a certain (one) intelligent' where یکی functions as an indefinite pronoun 'someone/a certain one' and هوشمند is the adjective 'intelligent' describing that person.
segments.s165-g574: که begins a relative clause modifying 'هوشمند' ('who' / 'whose'). رایش is 'his opinion/counsel' (رای + -ش 'his'). خرد here is a noun 'wisdom' (not 'small'); بود is past copula 'was.' و links the two predicates; دانش بلند = 'knowledge/learning (دانش) lofty/high (بلند)'. So: 'whose counsel was wise and whose learning was lofty.'
segments.s165-g575: که‌ای is a vocative particle in older/literary Persian (equivalent to 'که ای' = 'O'); دایه = 'nurse'; بچه شیرنر is a compound 'bache-ye shirnar' meaning 'lion-cub' (بچه = young/child, شیرنر = male lion / lion). Together: a direct address 'O nurse of the lion-cub.'
segments.s165-g576: چه رنجی is an exclamation 'what suffering' (چه used exclamatorily). که introduces the clause 'that ...'. جان = 'life' (here the object). هم = 'also/even.' نیاری is an archaic/poetic 2nd-person present-negative form meaning 'you do not bring' (compare later نیاوری); it functions as the verb. به بر = 'to the bosom/into the arms' (به + بر = toward/on the breast). The clause means roughly 'what suffering that you do not even bring life into your arms.'
segments.s165-g1105: چو introduces a temporal subordinate clause meaning “when.” The phrase یک بهره از شهر خرم is a noun phrase with the preposition از before شهر (i.e. “a portion/part of the joyful city”), and the verb بدید (archaic past of دیدن with the prefixed ب-) appears at the end as the third‑person past (“he saw/caught sight of”).
segments.s165-g1106: به marks the goal/direction (“to/toward”), followed by the coordinated noun phrase ایوان و باغ سیاوش (“Siavash’s iwan and garden” — باغ with the following name is possessive). رسید is the simple past third‑person singular “arrived/reached,” placed at the end of the clause.
segments.s165-g1107: به again marks direction (“toward”), followed by the noun کاخ فرنگیس (“Farangis’s palace”). بنهاد is an archaic past of نهادن (to place/set) used here in the compound رو نهادن/بنهادن meaning “to set/turn (one’s) face”; روی is the object “face.” Word order is: direction phrase + verb + object (classical/poetic ordering).
segments.s165-g1108: چنان is an intensifier/adverb (“such/so”), followed by a string of adjectives شاد و پیروز و دیهیم modifying the noun جوی (stream). Adjectives precede the noun in Persian; the list of adjectives characterizes the stream as joyful, victorious, and rustic.
segments.s165-g1109: پذیره here denotes reception/acceptance. The clitic in شدش is attached to شد and refers back to the masculine object pronoun “him” (classical clitic -ش). The nominal phrase دختر شهریار is the subject ‘the king’s daughter.’ The verb phrase is fronted in poetic word order, so the plain reading is “the king’s daughter received him.”
segments.s165-g1110: بپرسید is past third‑person “(she) asked” (probably ‘asked about him’); و links the actions. دینار is the object (coin/money). کردش is کرد plus the object clitic -ش (“did/made for him” → here “gave to him”), and نثار after it indicates that the dinars were given as an offering/gift. The clitic -ش again refers to the same masculine him.
segments.s165-g1111: چو introduces a temporal clause “when.” بر تخت بنشست is a prepositional phrase plus the archaic past beneshast (بنشست) ‘sat upon the throne.’ و connects the next verb-clause; آن جای دید places آن جای (“that place”) before the verb دید (‘he saw’), following normal Persian verb‑final order in this poetic line.
segments.s165-g1112: بران سان is a locative/qualifying phrase meaning “thereon/thereupon/like that” (بران = 'on/there', سان = 'such/as'), introducing the description. بهشتی is an adjective “heavenly,” دلارای is a compound adjective (دل‌آرا, lit. ‘heart‑adorning’) meaning “charming/heart‑delighting,” and دید is the final verb “he saw/beheld.” Together they mean he beheld a heavenly, heart‑pleasing sight.
segments.s165-g1113: 'بدان' is the older fused prepositional phrase (ب + آن) meaning 'to/at that' (here functioning adverbially 'to that/thereupon'); 'نیز' is an adverb meaning 'also'; 'چندی' is an adverbial phrase 'for a while'; 'ستایش گرفت' is a light-verb construction where the past verb گرفت (3rd-person singular) combines with the noun ستایش to mean 'took up/expressed praise' (i.e. 'praised' or 'offered praise').
segments.s165-g1114: 'جهان‌آفرین را' is the direct object marked by را ('the Creator' as definite object); 'نیایش گرفت' repeats the light-verb pattern with نیایش (prayer) as object and گرفت (3rd-singular past) to mean 'he offered/prayed' (i.e. 'took up prayer'). The verb is past tense and the object is definite by را.
segments.s165-g1115: 'ازان پس' (از آن پس) is a temporal phrase 'after that'; 'بخوردن گرفتند' is a periphrastic verbal construction where the past plural گرفتند plus the infinitive بخوردن yields 'they began to eat / they took to eating'; 'کار' here is a noun meaning 'the affair/matter' and with گرفتن often indicates 'the business/act of' (i.e. 'the business of eating').
segments.s165-g1116: This is a nominal list that functions as the object/complements of the previous verb: می‌و خوان (wine and banquet/feast), رامشگر (a comforter/entertainer), و میگسار (and cupbearer). The coordinating و links the items; these nouns are presented without an explicit verb because they complete 'began the business of ...'.
segments.s165-g1117: 'ببودند' is an older past-form meaning 'they were/they spent (time)'; 'یک هفته' gives the duration 'for a week'; 'با می‌به دست' is a prepositional phrase 'with mi-be (wine-and-quince) in hand'—می‌به is a poetic compound and دست is literal 'hand' indicating they held/possessed it during that time.
segments.s165-g1118: The paired adverbs 'گهی ... گاه ...' mean 'now... now...' or 'sometimes... sometimes...'; خرم و شاددل are adjectives 'joyful and cheerful' describing their state at one time, and مست 'drunk' describes the alternate state. Adjectives follow the adverbials and the copula is omitted (typical in classical Persian).
segments.s165-g1119: 'به هشتم' is a temporal prepositional phrase 'on the eighth (day)'; 'ره‌آورد' is the object 'tribute/gift' to be presented; 'پیش آورید' is an imperative (2nd-person plural) 'bring forward / bring before (him)', where پیش means 'in the presence of / before'. The verb آورید is the command 'bring'.
segments.s165-g1120: 'همان هدیه شارستان' is a determiner + noun + genitive phrase 'that same gift/offering of the city'; 'چون سزید' is a short clause meaning 'as was fitting / as was deserved' — چون here 'as', and سزید (3rd-singular past of سزیدن) 'was fitting/deserved'. The whole segment specifies which tribute and qualifies it as appropriate.
segments.s165-g729: به + شهری is a prepositional phrase 'to a city' (به = direction). که introduces a relative clause modifying شهری. آرام و رای are coordinated nouns 'calm and counsel', and آیدت is the verb 'comes to you' with the enclitic -ت marking 'to you'; here the present-form آید is used in an optative/wishing sense ('may ... come'). Word order is typical Persian: relative clause follows the head noun.
segments.s165-g730: همان آرزوها is the noun phrase 'those very wishes' (همان = 'the same/that very'). بجای here is part of the idiom بجای آمدن meaning 'to come into place / be fulfilled'; آیدت again 'comes to you' with the 2nd‑person clitic. The phrase as a whole expresses that those wishes will 'come to pass' for you.
segments.s165-g731: به شادی بباش and به نیکی بمان are optative/imperative formulas: به + noun ('in/with joy', 'in goodness') followed by the verbs بباش (be) and بمان (remain). These are hortatory commands wishing the addressee to 'be joyful' and 'stay in goodness'. The prepositional به gives an adverbial sense ('in/with').
segments.s165-g732: ز خوبی = 'from/of goodness' (ز = poetic 'from/because of'). مپرداز is a negative imperative (م‑ prefix + پرداز) meaning 'do not cast/throw away' or 'do not let go'. دل is the object 'heart', and یک زمان is an adverbial 'one moment' (i.e. 'not even for a moment'). The whole line warns: 'do not let your heart depart from goodness even for a moment.'
segments.s165-g733: سیاوش is the subject. ز گفتار او is an oblique/cause phrase 'because of/from his speech' (ز = 'because of/from', او = 'his'). گشت شاد is the verb + predicative adjective 'became happy' (گشت = became, شاد = happy). The past verb گشت indicates a past result: 'Siavash became glad at his words.'
segments.s165-g734: بزد is a simple past verb 'he struck/played'. نای و کوس lists the instruments 'ney (flute) and drum' as its objects. و بنه برنهاد continues with another past verb phrase: بنه (a small bundle/thing here) and برنهاد 'he raised/placed up'. The sequence lists musical action and then raising something, both in past narration.
segments.s165-g735: This segment is an enumerated noun phrase listing the items/forces: سلیح (weapons/armaments), سپاه (army/host), نگین (gems), کلاه (hats). The conjunction و simply links the list elements; the whole noun sequence functions as the direct object of the following verb.
segments.s165-g736: ببردند is past-plural 'they took/carried'. زین‌گونه 'thus/in this manner' modifies the action. با او 'with him' and به راه 'on the road / onto the way' are adverbial phrases describing accompaniment and direction. The clause means 'they took these things and in this way accompanied him on the road.'
segments.s165-g785: This is a reporting clause: 'سیاوش بدو گفت' = 'Siyâvash said to him'. 'بدو' = the enclitic/almost-pronominal prepositional phrase 'to him' (dative). 'کای' is a vocative marker 'O' addressing someone directly, followed by the addressee name 'بختیار'. Word order is Subject + indirect object + verb + vocative + name.
segments.s165-g786: This is the quoted predication about the addressee. 'درخت بزرگی' = 'a great/large tree' (noun + nominalized adjective), 'تو' = 'you' (addressed), and the final phrase 'آری به بار' expresses bearing/fruitfulness; here 'آری' functions as an affirmative/predicative element ('are' / 'indeed') and 'به بار' is the idiomatic part meaning 'bearing (fruit)'. Word order is poetic but corresponds to 'You are a large tree that bears fruit.'
segments.s165-g787: Poetic possession/attribution: 'مرا گنج و خوبی همه زان تست' places 'مرا' (to/for me / emphatic 'as for me') before the goods for emphasis. 'گنج و خوبی همه' = 'all treasure and goodness', 'زان' = 'from that / because of that', and the final form 'تست' here functions as a copular/predicate element indicating attribution to the addressee (archaic/poetic form). The clause as a whole says these goods are (due) to you/from that cause.
segments.s165-g788: 'به هر جای رنج تو بینم نخست' = 'in/at every place of your suffering I see (it) first.' 'به هر جای' = 'in every place/wherever', 'رنج تو' = 'your suffering' (noun + possessive), 'بینم' is first-person present/subjunctive-form of 'to see' used as 'I see' or 'I perceive', and 'نخست' = 'first/first of all' modifies the verb. The order places the locative phrase before the verb for emphasis.
segments.s165-g789: 'یکی' here functions as an indefinite/article-like 'a certain' or 'one', so 'یکی شهر سازم' = 'I will build a city.' 'سازم' is first-person singular present/future of 'ساختن' ('I build / I will build'). 'بدین' is the compound prepositional phrase 'به + این' = 'in/to this', followed by 'جای من' = 'my place' or 'this place of mine.' Word order: indefinite + noun + verb + locative phrase + possessor.
segments.s165-g790: 'که' introduces a subordinate clause of result/purpose: 'so that / which'. 'خیره بماند' = 'remain staring/astonished' — 'خیره' is an adjective 'astonished/staring' and 'بماند' (subjunctive/present form of 'ماندن') expresses 'be left / remain'. 'دل انجمن' = 'the heart(s) of the assembly' (possession expressed by juxtaposition in poetic order). The clause thus gives the intended effect of the city.
segments.s165-g791: 'ازان بوم خرم' = 'from that joyful land' (ablative + noun + adjective). 'چو' is the temporal conjunction 'when', and 'گشتند باز' is a past-tense verb phrase 'they returned / they turned back' (گشتند = they turned/went; باز = back/again). The temporal clause sets the time for what follows.
segments.s165-g792: 'سیاوش همی بود' uses the archaic/progressive 'همی + بود' to indicate continued state ('Siyâvash remained / was still'). 'با دل به راز' = 'with a heart in secret' (با = with; دل = heart; به راز = with a secret / secretly), describing his inner condition — he keeps his feelings hidden. The sequence shows his continuing, inward sorrow after others left.
segments.s165-g641: کجا (interrogative) is fronted to ask 'where'. بود is the past tense copula 'was' (here marking a question about location). کدبانوی پهلوان is a compound nominal phrase: کدبانوی (kadbānū, 'lady/mistress of the house') + پهلوان (’champion’) — in classical/poetic word order the possessor follows or is juxtaposed without an explicit ezāfe, so the phrase means 'the champion’s lady'.
segments.s165-g642: This is a nominal sentence with an emphasis inversion typical of poetry: ستوده (adjective 'praised') modifies زنی ('a woman'); بود is the past copula 'was' linking subject and predicate; روشن‌روان is a compound adjective 'bright‑souled / radiant of spirit' placed after the verb as a predicate epithet. The structure literally reads 'praised a woman was, bright‑souled.'
segments.s165-g643: به گنج اندرون is a prepositional/locative phrase: به + گنج + اندرون = 'in/into the treasure (house), inside'. آنچ is the relative/indefinite 'that which' or 'what(ever)'. بد نامدار here functions as a compound description: بد + نامدار = 'bad‑named' → 'ill‑famed / notorious' (classical Persian often forms such compounds without a connecting ezāfe). In context the compound is a descriptive epithet attached to آنچ.
segments.s165-g644: گزیده is a past participle/adjective 'selected' placed before the noun(s) it qualifies (the noun is implied/understood in poetic listing). ز is the poetic form of 'از' meaning 'from' or 'of'. زربفت is a compound noun 'gold‑brocade' and چینی (here postpositive) qualifies it as 'Chinese' — هزار is a numeral quantifier 'a thousand' placed after the described item in this poetic word order to mean 'a thousand (pieces) of Chinese gold‑brocade.'
segments.s165-g645: زبرجد is a gemstone noun used attributively before طبقها (plates/trays) — 'peridot plates'. طبقها is the plural form (طبق + ها). و links the next coordinated noun: پیروزه modifies جام, giving 'turquoise cup/goblet'. The adjectives precede or directly qualify the following noun in this classical poetic order.
segments.s165-g646: پر از is an adjectival phrase 'full of' (پر here = 'full (of)', followed by the preposition از). نافه is an older/rare word here used in the sense 'scent/perfume‑container/essence' rather than the literal 'navel' (so 'nafa(h) of musk'). مشک = 'musk'. Then و coordinates a parallel phrase: پر (again 'full') with the noun عود (oud/agarwood); خام (postpositive adjective) means 'raw' — poetic ellipsis omits a repeated از before the second پر, so the pattern is 'full of X and full of Y (raw oud)'.
segments.s165-g647: دو افسر: the numeral 'two' precedes افسر. In this treasure context افسر means 'helmet' (classical usage), not 'officer'. پر از گوهر شاهوار = 'full of gems (گوهر) that are شاهوار' — شاهوار is an adjective meaning 'regal/lofty' modifying گوهر. The construction uses پر از + noun to indicate the helmets are encrusted with royal gems.
segments.s165-g648: This line continues the list. دو یاره: 'two yāra' (here best read as 'two attendants/companions' or two ornamental pieces — the exact lexical nuance varies in classical usage). The remainder is an asyndetic/elliptical inventory: یکی طوق و دو گوشوار — 'one (a) collar/necklace and two ear‑ornaments (earrings)'. The verb 'were/was' is omitted (typical in poetic listing), so the numerals and nouns stand in apposition/description to those two items/people.
segments.s165-g537: بگفت این و برخاست از پیش او is a compact clause: بگفت (perfect past) means 'he said' and is followed by این 'this' referring to the words just spoken. و is a simple coordinating 'and', and برخاست is a past action 'stood up/rose' (here 'rose from his seat' or 'got up'). از پیش او places the motion relative to the other person: از 'from' + پیش او 'in his presence / before him'.
segments.s165-g538: چو آگاه گشت از کم و بیش او: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. آگاه (adjective) + گشت (past of گشتن 'to become') forms 'became aware'. از here has the sense 'concerning/of' (he became aware of ...). کم و بیش is the fixed pair 'less and more' — here 'shortcomings and excesses' — with و joining them, and او is the possessor 'his'.
segments.s165-g539: به شادی بشد: به شادی is an adverbial phrase 'with joy / in joy'. بشد (ب + شد) is the past form meaning 'became' or 'was made' — here 'he became joyful'. تا here is causal/temporal 'so that/then' introducing the next event (he then proceeded to the court). بدرگاه شاه is a locative phrase 'to the king's court / into the king's presence' (بدرگاه = 'to the presence of').
segments.s165-g540: فرود آمد و برگشادند راه: فرود آمد is a two-word verbal phrase 'came down / descended' (فرود = descent, آمد = came). و = 'and'. برگشادند is a past plural verb 'they opened' (here passive/impersonal sense: 'the ways were opened' or 'they opened the way'). راه is the object 'the way(s)/path(s)'.
segments.s165-g541: همی بود بر پیش او یک زمان: همی بود is the epic past-continuous idiom 'he was / he remained' (همی gives durative force). بر پیش او is a prepositional phrase 'in his presence / before him' (بر + پیش او), and یک زمان 'for a while / for a moment' indicates duration.
segments.s165-g542: بدو گفت سالار نیکوگمان: بدو is 'to him' (the indirect object). گفت here functions as the speech verb 'said' (in the text presented it appears in a form listed with noun senses, but its role is the past speech verb). سالار is the speaker's title 'commander / chief', and نیکوگمان is an epithet attached to him ('well-disposed / of good judgment'). Word order is typical: indirect object + speech verb + speaker title + epithet.
segments.s165-g543: که چندین چه باشی به پیشم به پای is an interrogative series. که introduces the question ('who/what'), چندین here functions as 'how / in what manner' or 'what sort (of)', and چه باشی is the present/subjunctive form 'are you / will you be' (here 'are you' in a question). به پیشم 'before me / in my presence' and به پای 'at my feet' give the location/attitude — the whole phrase asks the visitor's identity and reason for being at the speaker's feet.
segments.s165-g544: چه خواهی به گیتی چه آیدت رای continues the questioning: چه خواهی 'what do you want / what will you have' (خواهی is 2nd person singular present/future of خواستن). به گیتی 'in the world' restricts the desire to worldly affairs. چه آیدت رای is an idiom 'what counsel/plan comes to you' — آیدت (it comes to you) + رای (counsel/opinion), asking what design or advice the man offers.
segments.s165-g561: بپروردیم is a past-tense, first-person-plural form (we reared/raised) used here with a royal/plural-we sense; the verb comes first (poetic V-front order). چون پدر در کنار is a simile + locative: چون = “like/as,” پدر = “father,” and در کنار = a two-word locative phrase “at the side/beside.” Together they mean “we raised (you) like a father at (our) side.”
segments.s165-g562: This clause has a wishful/optative flavor though it uses the simple verb آورد (brought/bring). همه شادی = “all joy(s),” آورد = “(may) bring” (here functioning as the verb of result/wish), and بخت تو بار literally stacks words meaning “fortune / your load” (بار = load/stock). The whole order is somewhat inverted for poetry: “may your fortune bring a load of all joy.”
segments.s165-g563: کنون = “now.” همچنین = “thus/likewise” (an instruction to do similarly). کدخدایی is a deverbal/noun phrase meaning the office or post of کدخدا (headman) and بساز is the imperative/command-form ‘make/appoint’ (بـ‑ prefix + ساز). The sequence is an imperative/request: “Now likewise make/appoint a headman.”
segments.s165-g564: This short clause gives a comment about dependence: به نیک و بد = “in good and bad / in prosperity and adversity.” از تو نیم بی‌نیاز is elliptical: نیم (literally “half/partially”) modifies بی‌نیاز (“not in need, independent”), with از تو marking the source/relatum (“of/from you”). Together it means “I am not wholly free/independent of you in good or bad.” Note the older/poetic ordering (particle + phrase + predicate) and the compressed negative sense conveyed by نیم + بی‌نیاز.
segments.s165-g565: پس = “then/so.” پرده here functions as a substantive meaning a veiled woman or woman who is behind the curtain (a conventional poetic expression for a married/veiled female). تو = “your.” یکی = “one / a certain.” دخترست is an older, fused form meaning “is a daughter” (دختر + است), so the clause states “So your veiled one is a daughter.”
segments.s165-g566: که = relative ‘who/that.’ ایوان و تخت = two nouns ‘portico/hall and throne’ joined by و. م (here representing the copula/ possessive context in the manuscript splitting) + را (object/ emphasis marker) + درخورست = ‘is fitting/appropriate.’ The clause thus says the daughter is fitting for (مرا/‘my’) portico and throne; word-order and the split copula/possessive are typical of older/poetic orthography.
segments.s165-g567: فرنگیس is the proper name (subject). خواند here means ‘called’ (not ‘read’); همی is an older continuous/iterative marker meaning ‘continually/kept on’; مادرش is ‘her mother’ (مادر + ـش possessive suffix). The clause literally: “Farangis kept calling (her) ‘mother.’”
segments.s165-g568: شود شاد = ‘will become glad’ (شود used as future/resultive auxiliary). اگر باشم = ‘if I am’ (اگر introduces the conditional; باشم is first-person singular subjunctive/potential). اندر خورش is a locative phrase ‘in/within [her] خورش’; here خورش functions as a possessive/household-related noun (= ‘her keeping/household/care’ in context), so the whole conditional reads: ‘she will be glad if I am in her care/household.’ Note the older locative marker اندر and the classical conditional syntax.
segments.s165-g521: چو is a conditional conjunction here meaning 'when/if'. از روی ایشان is a prepositional phrase literally 'from their face/presence' (روی = 'face/presence'), so the clause means 'withdraw from their presence'. بباید is a modal verb 'it is necessary / one must'; برید is the lexical verb meaning 'to withdraw/leave' in this poetic/elliptical use (classical Persian uses bare past-stem after باید to express necessity).
segments.s165-g522: به توران is a directional phrase 'to Turan'. همی is an archaic adverbial particle used with verbs in Ferdowsi; here it marks the narrative/affirmative sense (rendered 'then/indeed'). جای is the noun 'place', and the construction جای باید گزید uses باید + past stem (گزید) to express necessity: 'one must choose a place' (object before verb is normal in Persian).
segments.s165-g523: پدر باش is an imperative: 'be a father' (باش = imperative 'be'). و links the clauses. این کدخدایی بساز is another imperative: این + کدخدایی 'this headman-ship' with بساز 'make/establish' (here: 'assume' or 'take on' the office). The sequence is second-person imperatives joined by و.
segments.s165-g524: مگو is a negative imperative 'do not say / do not tell'. این سخن is the object 'this speech/matter'. با زمین literally 'with the land' here idiomatically means 'to the country/people' (do not tell the populace). جز به راز is an exception phrase 'except in secret' (جز = 'except', به راز = 'in secret').
segments.s165-g525: اگر بخت باشد is a conditional clause 'if fate/fortune be (favourable)'. The clause مرا نیکخواه is elliptical: مرا (the object pronoun 'me') with the noun نیکخواه 'well-wisher' implies 'I have a well-wisher' or 'there will be a friend for me'—the verb is omitted but understood from context (a common poetic ellipsis).
segments.s165-g526: همانا is an emphatic particle 'verily / behold'. دهد is third-person verbal form 'he gives/will give' (in classical usage this present form often carries an optative/future force). ره به پیوند شاه is the object phrase 'a path/way to a joining with the king' (ره 'way' governed by دهد, پیوند شاه 'union/bond with the king').
segments.s165-g527: همی is the archaic continuous/iterative particle used with past narration ('then/so'). گفت is the verbal root 'said' (here used narratively). مژگان پر از آب کرد is a causative/result clause: مژگان 'eyelashes' + پر از آب 'full of water' + کرد 'made/did' meaning 'she filled her eyelashes with water' (i.e., she filled her eyes with tears).
segments.s165-g528: همی again marks the narrative continuation. برزد is an archaic intransitive verb here 'to tremble/shake'. اندر میان باد سرد is a locative phrase 'in the midst of the cold wind' (اندر میان = 'amid/among', باد سرد = 'cold wind'), adverbially modifying the verb.
segments.s165-g977: سپهدار ترکان is a noun phrase meaning 'the commander of the Turks' (title + group) with the genitive relation expressed by juxtaposition typical of epic Persian. ز کردار خویش is a poetic prepositional phrase 'from/on account of his conduct': ز = 'from/because of', کردار = 'deed/behavior', خوی + ش are the possessive element ('one's own' + third‑person clitic 'his'), so together they mark 'his conduct'.
segments.s165-g978: پشیمان شود is a predicate: پشیمان 'regretful' + شود (شد in literary present) 'becomes/iscome', forming 'becomes regretful'. هم here means 'also/likewise', and ز گفتار خویش repeats the same structure as the previous segment: ز = 'because of/from', گفتار = 'speech/words', خوی + ش = 'his (own)', so the whole means 'also for his words.'
segments.s165-g979: پشیمانی 'regret' is the noun subject. آنگه 'then/in that case' sets the temporal/conditional frame. نداردش is ندارد + the clitic ش ('for him'/'to him'), yielding 'has/does not have for him'; سود 'benefit' names what is lacking. The archaic word order places the verb before the beneficiary clitic (نداردش سود = 'regret then has no benefit for him').
segments.s165-g980: که introduces a clause explaining why regret is useless ('for/when...'). برخیزد is the literary third‑person form 'rises/ascends' (verb before subject in poetic inversion). از بوم آباد = 'from the bûm (dwelling/roof) that is آباد (prosperous/inhabited)' and دود 'smoke' is the thing rising; the clause thus reads 'that smoke rises from the inhabited roof.'
segments.s165-g981: بیا is a hortative/imperative 'come' (here equivalent to 'let us'). تا introduces a purpose/hortative clause ('so that/let us ...'). به شادی is an adverbial 'in joy/in merriment' (به + noun), خوریم and دهیم are first‑person plural present/subjunctive forms 'let us eat' and 'let us give' coordinated by و, expressing a proposed action together.
segments.s165-g982: چو = 'when'. گاه 'time/occasion'. گذشتن is the verbal noun/infinitive 'to pass' (here 'time to pass/leave'), and بود is the copula 'was/there was' used in a literary sense to mean 'when the time to pass arrives'. بگذریم is first‑person plural subjunctive/optative 'let us pass/let us go', giving the resolution 'when the time comes, we will depart.'
segments.s165-g983: چو 'when' introduces a temporal clause. بشنید is the past form 'heard' (third person); پیران 'the elders' is the group affected/acting — Persian epic often shows variation in verb agreement. و اندیشه کرد is a common collocation meaning 'and (they/he) thought/pondered' (اندیشه 'thought' + کرد 'did/made' = 'pondered').
segments.s165-g984: ز = 'from/because of'. گفتار او = 'his speech/words' (گفتار 'speech' + او 'his'). شد is 'became' (copula of change). دلش = دل + clitic ش 'his heart'. پر 'full (of)' and ز درد 'with pain/sorrow' complete the predicate: 'his heart became full of sorrow because of his words.' Note the poetic order (verb before subject and object phrases).
segments.s165-g513: This conditional clause begins with اگر (if). آسمانی here is an adjective modifying رای (the 'counsel' or decree that comes from heaven), and چنین is an adverb meaning 'thus/so'. است is the copula 'is', so together: 'If the heavenly counsel is thus.' Word order is standard for a verbless descriptive clause in classical Persian: conditional marker + noun phrase + copula.
segments.s165-g514: رای مرا is a possessive/possessed phrase meaning 'my counsel' (مرا here marks the possessor after the noun in older/poetic Persian). با سپهر روان نیست پای is the predicate: با + سپهر روان 'with the moving/revolving heavens', نیست 'is not', and پای 'foot/standing' (i.e. 'has no footing/standing'). The negation applies to پای: 'has no standing with the moving sky.'
segments.s165-g515: The clause opens with اگر (if). من is the explicit subject 'I' (often optional in Persian). به ایران نخواهم رسید is a negative future construction: نخواهم (auxiliary 'I will not') + reached verb رسید (here used as the main verb 'reach/arrive'), so 'if I will not reach Iran' = 'if I do not reach Iran.' Word order is subject + object phrase + verb.
segments.s165-g516: نخواهم again expresses future negation 'I will not'; همی is a classical adverb meaning 'always/ever' or here giving emphasis ('ever/at all'); روی کاووس دید is the object 'the face of Kavus' + دید 'see/behold'. Together: 'I will not ever behold Kavus's face.' Note the auxiliary (نخواهم) precedes the main verb (دید), typical in classical lines.
segments.s165-g517: چو here means 'as' or 'like.' دستان in this poetic context functions as a personal name or reference (rather than the literal 'hands' or 'tales'), followed by a relative clause introduced by که. پروردگار is 'nurturer' or 'lord/protector', and منست is an archaic/contracted copula (literally 'is mine' / 'is my ...' in older usage). The clause therefore means 'as Dastān, who is my nurturer/protector.'
segments.s165-g518: This is a parallel relative phrase referring to تهمتن (Tahmatan, an epithet meaning 'mighty one'). که introduces the relative clause; روشن بهار is a poetic kenning 'bright spring' (metaphorically source of life or radiance), and منست again is the archaic/copulative element linking it to the speaker ('is of mine' / 'belongs to me' in poetic sense). Together: 'Tahmatan, who is the bright spring of mine.'
segments.s165-g519: چو ... چون are repeated particles meaning 'like' or 'as' used to list examples: 'like Bahram and like Zangah.' شاوران is the plural of 'advisor' (شاور), so the phrase groups those two names as advisers. The repetition of the comparative particle is a stylistic/poetic device.
segments.s165-g520: جزین means 'apart from these' or 'besides these.' نامدران is 'renowned/famous ones' and کنداوران is a plural agent noun (from کنداور) meaning either 'investigators' or literally 'diggers/excavators' — in poetic lists such an agent-word can mean 'those who delve or probe (i.e. investigators).' The sequence lists additional notable persons 'besides these famed investigators.'
segments.s165-g473: بیاراست is a past third‑person singular of بیاراستن ('to adorn'); the subject is omitted (understood from context). او را is the object marked by the accusative/definiteness particle را. چو introduces a simile ('like/as'), followed by خرم بهار ('the joyful spring') describing how he was dressed. Word order here is verb → object → simile, typical of classical/poetic Persian.
segments.s165-g474: فرستاد is past third‑person singular 'he sent' (subject again omitted and understood). در شب is a temporal adverbial ('in/at night'); بر شهریار uses بر in the dative/toward sense ('to the king') — classical/poetic usage where بر = 'toward/to'. The clause describes the action and its time and recipient.
segments.s165-g475: مراو here is an irregular/archaic pronominal form referring to the person being moved (a fused/poetic pronoun); it takes را as the object marker. بپیوست (past third singular of پیوستن with the prefix ب‑) means 'he joined/united' and takes با شاه نو ('with/to the new king') to indicate whom she was joined to. The verb's subject is omitted and understood from the narrative context.
segments.s165-g476: نشاند is an archaic/poetic past transitive meaning 'he seated/placed (someone)'. از بر گاه is a compound prepositional/locative phrase (از + بر + گاه) used poetically to mean 'at the side/by the seat/throne' — here indicating where she was seated. چون ماه نو is a simile ('like the new moon') modifying how she was placed. Word order: verb → locative phrase → simile.
segments.s165-g477: ندانست is the negative past (he/they did not know); here it functions impersonally with کس ('no one') placed after the verb in classical word order (verb first, then noun). گنج او را is the direct object phrase 'his treasure' (او را: 'his/her' + object marker را). شمار functions as noun/abstract ('the counting/number'), so the clause reads 'No one knew the count/number of his treasure.'
segments.s165-g478: ز introduces material/source ('of/from') in a poetic register; the two coordinated prepositional phrases ز یاقوت و ز تاج گوهرنگار enumerate what the treasure consisted of ('of ruby and of jeweled crown'). گوهرنگار is an adjectival/compound noun meaning 'jeweled' or 'adorned with gems' modifying تاج.
segments.s165-g479: سیاوش is the subject (proper name). چو here functions temporally as 'when' (classical/poetic usage). روی جریره is an object phrase 'the face of Jariereh' (روی = face). بدید is past tense 'saw' (b‑ prefix is a poetic variant of the past). The clause therefore means 'When Siyâvash saw Jariereh's face...'.
segments.s165-g480: خوش آمدش is an impersonal/idiomatic construction: خوش آمد ('it pleased') + enclitic ش (to him) = 'he was pleased' or 'he liked her'. خندید is past 'he laughed'; و coordinates the following clause; شادی گزید is literal 'he chose joy' (گزید, past of گزیدن, used here in the sense 'to choose/embrace'). The three verbs describe his emotional response.
segments.s165-g369: بدان is a contracted prepositional phrase (بِ + آن) meaning 'to that'; it front-loads the indirect object ('to that prince'). چنین گفت is a formulaic sequence 'said thus' with گفت as a past-tense verb. شاه appears after the verb for emphasis/identification — 'the king said'.
segments.s165-g370: که introduces the reported clause (complementizer 'that' / content clause). یک روز is a temporal adverbial 'one day' (meaning 'one time' or 'sometime'). با من به نخچیرگاه is a prepositional phrase 'with me to the hunting-ground' giving the intended accompaniment and destination; the main verb of the clause is understood from the context (the speech continues in following lines).
segments.s165-g371: گر is the conditional 'if'. آیی is the 2nd-person present/subjunctive form 'you come' or 'you should come'. که here introduces purpose/result ('so that' or 'in order that'). دل شاد و خرم کنیم is a purposive clause: دل (heart) + two adjectives (شاد, خرم) and کنیم 'we make' (subjunctive/intentional 'may/let us make'), meaning 'that we may make (your/our) hearts glad and joyful'.
segments.s165-g372: روان را is the definite object marker (را) attached to روان 'spirit/soul', marking it as the thing affected. به نخچیر is a locative/directional phrase 'at/to the hunt'. بی‌غم کنیم repeats the verb کنیم 'we will make' with the adjective بی‌‌غم 'carefree' as result—'make the spirit carefree'. The structure shows coordinated results: make heart glad and make spirit carefree at the hunt.
segments.s165-g373: بدو is the prepositional clitic بَه + او = 'to him' (indirect object). گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (here marking reported speech). هرگه که is a temporal/conditional 'whenever/if ever' and رای آیدت = 'a plan/counsel comes to you' where رای is the noun 'plan/counsel' and آیدت is 'comes to you' (آید + enclitic ت).
segments.s165-g374: بران سو is a deictic phrase 'to that side/direction' (بران = 'to/on that', سو = 'side/direction'). که introduces the relative/purpose clause 'which/that ...'. دل رهنمای is a genitival compound 'the heart's guide' (رهنمای = guide/adviser attached to دل). آیدت again is 'comes to you' — here meaning 'which your heart's guide points out to you'. The verb 'go' is implied by the context (imperative sense).
segments.s165-g375: برفتند is the simple past plural 'they went/departed'. روزی here is the temporal adverb 'one day' (a narrative temporal marker). به نخچیرگاه is the destination 'to the hunting-ground'. The clause sets the scene in the past narrative.
segments.s165-g376: همی is a classical-progressive particle (marks ongoing/habitual aspect or emphasis) so همی رفت is an imperfective/past continuous 'he/it was going' or emphatic past 'he went'. با یوز و با باز is an instrumental construction 'with a cheetah and with a falcon' (both hunting animals). شاه at the end identifies the subject 'the king' (postverbal subject for emphasis).
segments.s165-g689: بدین کار is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in/for this work' (بدین = به + این). بگذشت is a past-tense impersonal/3rd-person verb 'passed' used with a time expression; here 'یک هفته' is the duration (one week) that follows the verb. نیز ‘also/too’ adds the sense that this week was additional/emphasized.
segments.s165-g690: سپهبد (title) is the subject placed before the verb; بیاراست is a past-tense verb 'adorned/prepared' (classical form). بسیار چیز is the object phrase 'many things' (بسیار modifying چیز); word order is S–V–O.
segments.s165-g691: The repeated از marks source/stock: 'from the ...'. اسپان تازی is a compound meaning 'Arabian horses/steeds' (اسپان = horses, تازی qualifies them as Tazi/Arabian). The second از introduces a second source 'from the sheep.'
segments.s165-g692: همان here means 'the same/those same' and introduces a list of items. جوشن, تیغ, کمند are nouns 'cuirass/armor', 'sword', 'lasso' joined by و. خود here occurs in the list between jošn and تیغ; its meaning in this context is likely an item name (e.g. a kind of shield/armour), but the provided lemma senses are not a match, so its exact sense is ambiguous in the supplied senses.
segments.s165-g693: ز (poetic variant of از) marks source/possession: 'from dinars'. The phrase continues with و ('and') and another از introducing another coin-type. بدرهای is uncertain in the provided senses and does not match a clear monetary sense here, so its sense is left as unknown; درم is 'dirham' (coin).
segments.s165-g694: ز پوشیدنیها means 'from the garments/clothing' (پوشیدنیها = wearables). The following phrase از بیش و کم is an idiomatic pair meaning 'of more and less' or 'great and small' — i.e. things of every sort; بیش and کم form a contrastive pair.
segments.s165-g695: وزین modifies مرز ('the weighty/dignified border') and functions as an adjective before the noun. تا is used in the sense 'as far as' (extent), and پیش دریای چین means 'before/in front of the Sea of China' — چین here denotes the region/China.
segments.s165-g696: همی is a classical particle often signalling continuous or habitual action ('kept/was continually'); نام بردند is the fixed collocation 'to name/mention/enumerate' in the past plural. شهر و زمین are the direct objects 'cities and lands'.
segments.s165-g193: بدان شب هم اندر بفرمود شاه: "بدان شب" is a prepositional + demonstrative phrase meaning "on that night/that same night" (ب = to/at; دان here forms the demonstrative). هم = "also/too." اندر = "in/within/at." بفرمود is the honorific past verb (he ordered/said), with شاه (king) as the agent; word order places the temporal phrase and adverb before the verb and subject, typical of classical Persian narrative.
segments.s165-g194: بدان کس که بودند بر بزمگاه: بدان کس = "to/at that person(s)" (demonstrative + noun); که introduces a relative clause "who/that"; بودند is the past plural of بودن "they were/there were"; بر بزمگاه = "at/on the banquet-hall/assembly." The whole phrase functions as the object/indirect object of بفرمود (he commanded/ordered those who were at the feast).
segments.s165-g195: چنین گفت با شیده افراسیاب: چنین گفت = "thus (he) said/said thus" (چنین = thus; گفت here functions as 'said' in narrative). با شیده + افراسیاب is an appositive/circumstantial descriptor: با شیده (= "being love‑struck/maddened by love") describes Afrasiab; the proper name follows, so the phrase reads as "the love‑struck Afrasiab said thus."
segments.s165-g196: که چون سر برآرد سیاوش ز خواب: که introduces the subordinate clause ("that/when"); چون here gives the temporal sense "when/as soon as"; سر برآرد is a classical present/imperfect third‑person singular (برآرد = "raises/lifts up") describing Siavash's action; ز خواب = "from sleep." The clause is a dependent temporal clause completing گفت (what was said).
segments.s165-g197: تو با پهلوانان و خویشان من: تو = "you" (address); با introduces companionship "with" and coordinates پهلوانان (champions) and خویشان (kinsmen). من placed after the coordinated nouns marks possession "my champions and my kinsmen." The phrase lists the set in which the addressee is included.
segments.s165-g198: کسی کاو بود مهتر انجمن: کسی = "anyone/someone"; کاو is the archaic/poetic relative (here functioning like "who/that/of whom") linking کسی to the rest of the clause; بود is the past of بودن "was"; مهتر انجمن = "the chief/head of the assembly" (adjective/title before the noun). Together the sequence means "someone who was the assembly's chief."
segments.s165-g199: به شبگیر با هدیه و با غلام: به + شبگیر gives the temporal/locative sense "at night/at nightfall" (شبگیر = nocturnal or night‑visitor used adverbially); با هدیه و با غلام is a parallel prepositional series "with gifts and with slaves/attendants," where با is repeated for emphasis/clarity.
segments.s165-g200: گرانمایه اسپان زرین ستام: گرانمایه (precious/valued) modifies اسپان (steeds/horses); زرین (golden) modifies the following noun ستام, so the string reads as stacked epithets and nouns meaning "precious steeds and golden [trappings/praise]" — contextually best understood as "precious horses with golden trappings." Adjectives precede the nouns they modify in this poetic construction.
segments.s165-g337: This clause is a concise past-tense action: یکی (indefinite 'someone') is the subject, تیر is the direct object and زد is the past-tense verb 'shot/struck'. The prepositional phrase بر میان نشان ('at/on the middle mark') follows the verb to indicate the aim/place. Word order is subject — object — verb with a post-verbal locative phrase (typical older/poetic Persian).
segments.s165-g338: نهاده is the perfect/past participle (from نهادن) used as a finite past-action 'placed' or 'having placed'. بدو is the clitic/prepositional pronoun 'to it / upon it' referring back to 'نشان'. چشم here is used metaphorically as 'gaze/eye' (not only the physical organ), and گردنکشان is an epithet 'the proud/neck‑raisers' (i.e. proud men). Together the phrase means 'having placed upon it the gaze of the proud ones' — i.e. 'the proud fixed their eyes on it.'
segments.s165-g339: This nominal phrase names the thing/agent (خدنگی — literally 'dagger' but possibly a proper/poetic name) followed by the adverbial compound دگر باره 'once more/again'. با چارپر is a prepositional phrase 'with Charpar' (Charpar as a name or epithet). The phrase sets up the participant and circumstantial detail before the verbal clause that follows.
segments.s165-g340: بینداخت is a past tense (or preterite) meaning 'threw/cast' (here passive/active reading depends on context); از باد 'by/from the wind' gives the cause or means ('driven by the wind' or 'from a gust'). The coordinating و joins بگشاد پر, where بگشاد is a past-form 'opened/spread' and پر (here 'feather/wing') is its object: 'and it opened/spread its wing.' The subject of both verbs is the same thing named in the previous segment (implied).
segments.s165-g341: نشانه دوباره is 'the target / mark, again' (noun + adverb). به یک تاختن is an adverbial/instrumental phrase: به + numeral + verbal noun (تاختن 'to charge') functions as 'by/with one charge' or 'in a single onrush'. The construction uses the gerund/infinitival verbal noun to indicate manner.
segments.s165-g342: مغربل (literally 'sieve') is used metaphorically as the object/result; بکرد (from کردن) is the past-tense auxiliary-like verb 'made/did' taking مغربل as its complement: 'made (it) a sieve'. اندر + انداختن ('into/to throw') is an infinitival/directional phrase meaning 'to cast into' or 'to throw in'. Together the clause expresses that by that single charge he rendered the target as if sifted and threw it in — a compact, poetic way to describe destructive effect.
segments.s165-g343: عنان را is the definite object with the object marker را ('the reins'). بپیچید is past tense 'he twisted/turned' (بـ + پیچید, here perfective). بر دست راست is a locative phrase 'onto/into (his) right hand' (بر = onto, دست راست = right hand). The object marker plus verb plus locative produce 'he twisted the reins onto his right hand.'
segments.s165-g344: بزد is the simple past 'he struck/struck out'. بار دیگر ('once more', literally 'another time') is an adverbial expression modifying بزد. بران سو is a prepositional phrase 'toward that side' (بران = 'thereon/thence', سو = 'side/toward'). The relative clause که خواست ('which he wanted') follows and restricts سو: 'toward that side which he wished.'
segments.s165-g1097: بدو (bedo) is the prepositional clitic 'to him/unto him' placed before the verb phrase; گفت‌گر is an archaic, poetic form meaning 'said he' (literally 'speaker/said one') and introduces direct speech. فر و برز کیان is a coordinated noun phrase 'the Kians' فر (divine/royal glory) and برز (exaltation/high estate)', placed after the verb phrase as the topic of the conditional clause.
segments.s165-g1098: نبودیت is an archaic/poetic verbal form (conditional/optative nuance) meaning 'would not have been' or 'were not'; here it predicates the noun phrase that precedes it. با دانش is an instrumental phrase 'by/with wisdom', and اندر جهان is the locative adverbial 'in the world' — together they give the cause/location: 'would not have been by wisdom in the world.'
segments.s165-g1099: کی here is the interrogative 'when'. آغاز کردی is a past tense construction 'you began' (آغاز + کردی). بدین گونه = 'in this manner/this way' (بدین = به + این, گونه = manner), and جای ('place/seat') is the object/topic: 'When did you begin, in this manner, to occupy/take this place?'. Note the classical order puts the temporal interrogative before the verbal phrase.
segments.s165-g1100: کجا آمدی is the second interrogative clause 'where did you come'. جای here repeats 'this place/spot'. زین سان ('زین' = saddle; 'سان' = like/so) + به پای ('to the foot' / 'to the ground') describes manner of arrival: 'coming from the saddle to the ground' — i.e. dismounting. The phrase as a whole asks the place/source and manner of his coming.
segments.s165-g1101: بماناد is an optative/imperative-like archaic form 'may it remain' (wish or blessing) placed at the start. تا رستخیز 'until Resurrection' gives the temporal limit. این نشان 'this token/sign' is the thing wished to remain (object/topic). Word order places the optative verb before its temporal complement and the direct object.
segments.s165-g1102: میان is the preposition 'among/between'. دلیران is the plural 'the brave/valiant ones' and گردنکشان is a coordinated plural 'and the گردنکشان' (here 'rebels' or 'proud ones'); the entire prepositional phrase specifies among whom the token should remain.
segments.s165-g1103: پسر بر پسر is an idiomatic expression 'son upon son' (successive generations or many sons). همچنین 'thus/likewise' modifies the wish. شاد باد is the optative formula 'may (they) be happy' where باد is the optative particle (not the noun 'wind'); the verbal sense is expressed elliptically (the predicate 'be' is conveyed by باد).
segments.s165-g1104: A string of epithets in apposition: جهاندار 'world-ruler/sovereign', پیروز 'victorious', و فرخ نژاد 'and of fortunate lineage' — these are predicative descriptions tied to the wished-for sons; the coordination (و) links the adjectives and the compound noun 'فرخ نژاد' (fortunate + lineage).
segments.s165-g777: بدو (be-do) is the dative/allative phrase 'to him' placed before the verb for emphasis. گفت is the past tense verb 'said' (here introducing direct speech). پیران is the speaker’s name/title; که‌ای (a poetic sequence of که + ای) here functions as a vocative 'O you who are...' and is followed by the adjective + noun خوب رای 'good counsel' addressing the addressee.
segments.s165-g778: بران رو is a locative/ directive phrase: بران 'to/onto that' + رو 'toward/face' → 'go toward that place'. که here introduces purpose/result ('so that' / 'that'), اندیشه is the noun 'thought', آرد is the present/future-subjunctive-like form 'brings' (literary Persian uses آرد for 'may bring' / 'brings'), and بجای means 'in place' / 'instead (thereof)'.
segments.s165-g779: چو here = 'when/if' (a conditional/temporal conjunction). فرمان 'command' is the noun object of دهد 'gives/issues' (دهد is present/future simple 'gives' or literary 'would give/issuing'). من is the subject 'I'. بران سان = بران 'in/onto that' + سان 'manner/so' → 'in that way'. که خواست links as a relative/purpose clause 'that he wished' or 'as he wishes', where خواست is the noun/verb 'wish/desire' (literary sense).
segments.s165-g780: برآرم is the 1st-person singular present/future-subjunctive 'I will raise/lift up' (literary conjugation). یکی جای 'one place/seat' is the direct object. تا introduces purpose/result here = 'so that/in order that'. ماه is used metaphorically 'the moon' (often a beautiful person); راست is an adverb/adjective 'straight/upright' or 'rightly' describing how the moon/place should be set.
segments.s165-g781: نخواهم is the negative of خواهم 'I want' → 'I do not want' (present/future sense). که introduces a subordinate clause 'that ... should be'. باشد is the subjunctive/present form 'should be' (literary). مرا (split here as م + را) is the dative/accusative 'to me / for me' (م is orthographic here as part of مرا; را is the object marker). بوم is 'homeland/home' and گنج is 'treasure/wealth'. The clause states a wish against possessing homeland and treasure.
segments.s165-g782: زمان و زمین 'time and land/earth' are listed possessions. از تو دارم is literally 'I have (them) from you' or 'I possess, thanks to you' (دارم is present 'I have'). سپنج is a literal 'sponge' — here used metaphorically (poetically) and stands at the end of the line; its exact metaphorical force is literary/elliptical.
segments.s165-g783: یکی شارستان سازم: یکی 'one/a single', شارستان 'city/town', سازم is 1st-person present/future 'I will build/make' (literary). ایدر is a poetic form meaning 'here', and فراخ is an adjective 'wide/spacious' modifying شارستان (postpositive in Persian poetry).
segments.s165-g784: فراوان 'abundantly' or 'plentiful', followed by بدو 'to it' (i.e., to that city). اندر is the literary preposition 'in/within', introducing locations ایوان و کاخ 'halls and palaces' as the places to be filled. The word order (adverb → indirect object → prepositional phrase) is typical of Persian poetic compression.
segments.s165-g489: تو دانی که = 'you know that' (تو + دانی, present 2nd-sg.). The following noun group سالار توران سپاه is a compressed poetic noun phrase meaning 'the commander (سالار) of Turan's army': Persian order is here inverted/condensed for verse (سالار + possessor + possessed).
segments.s165-g490: ز اوج فلک = 'from the apex/height of the sky' (ز + اوج + فلک). برفرازد is 3rd-sg. present (poetic) verb with the prefix بر- 'lift up/exalt'; کلاه is the object 'hat/helmet'. Word order (verb before object) and the hyperbolic locative make this poetic.
segments.s165-g491: شب و روز روشن روانش توی = 'night and day his bright spirit is in/within (you)'. روشن modifies روانش ('his radiant spirit'). توی is the locative 'in/within' placed at the end for emphasis; the precise possessive relation is completed in the next segment.
segments.s165-g492: دل و هوش و توش و توانش توی = a coordinated list ('heart and intellect and his inner being and his strength') followed by توی 'in/within'. The nouns are linked by و; توش (literally 'in it' / 'his inner part') and توانش ('his power') carry the possessive suffix -ش referring back to 'روانش' (his spirit).
segments.s165-g493: چو با او تو پیوسته خون شوی = 'if/when you (تو) are constantly (پیوسته) blood (خون) with him (با او)'. چو introduces a conditional ('if'); the order با او تو (with him, you) is inverted for rhythm. شوی is present/subjunctive 'become/are' used in conditional sense: 'become blood' = share blood/be blood-bonded.
segments.s165-g494: ازین پایه هر دم به افزون شوی = 'from this basis/point (از این پایه) every moment (هر دم) you will become more (به افزون شوی)'. ازین is the fused poetic form of 'از این'. به افزون functions adverbially 'increasingly/more', and شوی again expresses the resulting change (you will become).
segments.s165-g495: بباشد امیدش به تو استوار = 'may his hope be firm in you' — بَباشد is the optative/subjunctive 'may it be' (بـ + باشد). امیدش 'his hope' and به تو استوار 'firm upon you' form the wished-for content.
segments.s165-g496: که خواهی بدن پیش او پایدار = 'so that / for you will (خواهی) keep your body (بدن) steady (پایدار) before him (پیش او)'. که introduces the result/explanatory clause. خواهی here functions as a future/auxiliary 'you will', and the predicate places 'بدن' as the theme whose steadiness is asserted in his presence.
segments.s165-g161: "که" introduces a subordinate/exclamatory clause ('who/that'); "بشکیبد" is an older jussive/subjunctive/imperative form of شکیبیدن meaning 'be patient' (here hypothetical: 'could/should be patient'); "از روی" is a prepositional phrase literally 'from the face' = 'at the sight of'; "چونین پسر" = 'such a son' (چونین postpositive adjective 'such'). Word order is typical of classical Persian: verb (بشکیبد) appears before the modifying prepositional phrase.
segments.s165-g162: "چنین" is an intensifier here ('so' / 'such'); "برز بالا" are stacked adjectives/epithets meaning 'exalted/high' or 'very tall' (classical poetic doubling to strengthen the sense of loftiness); "و" = 'and'; "چندین هنر" = 'many/various arts or skills' (چندین = several; هنر = skill, excellence). The phrase gives the qualities of the aforementioned 'پسر'.
segments.s165-g163: "مرا" (here marked by the two tokens م + را) functions as the experiencer/possessive ('to me' / 'my'); "دیده" is 'eye' or 'sight' (the affected faculty); "از خوب دیدار او" is a causal/benefactive prepositional phrase 'because of / at the lovely sight of him' (از + adjective + noun + pronoun). The phrase is a nominal prelude to the verb that follows in the next segment.
segments.s165-g164: "بمانده‌ست" is a perfect/resultative compound meaning 'has remained / is left' (here: 'remains/stays in that condition'); "دل" is the grammatical subject 'the heart'; "خیره" is an adjectival/state word meaning 'staring / astonished'; "از کار او" is a causal phrase 'because of his deed(s)'. Together they state the resulting emotional state caused by the sight described earlier.
segments.s165-g165: "که" again introduces the subordinate idea ('that'); "فرزند باشد" uses باشد in the subjunctive/potential sense 'be/should be' (here: 'there should be / there be a child'); "کسی را" is a dative/indirect-object phrasing 'to someone' (کسی = someone; را used as emphasis/beneficiary); "چنین" follows and modifies فرزند ('such a child'). The clause expresses the hypothetical/worthy nature of having such a child.
segments.s165-g166: "دو دیده" is the noun phrase 'two eyes' (subject of the following verb); "بگرداند" is the causative/active form of گردانیدن meaning 'turns' / 'causes to go about' (here 'make/let the eyes sweep/roam'); "اندر زمین" is an adverbial locative 'throughout/on the earth' (اندر = in/within). The verb governs the motion of the eyes across the world, completing the thought about the child's effect.
segments.s165-g167: "از ایوان‌ها" is a prepositional phrase 'from the porticos/iwans' (ایوان‌ها plural); "پس" here is temporal/sequence 'then/afterwards'; "یکی" 'one' (a single one); "برگزید" is past tense 'he chose/selected'. The implied object of برگزید is 'one (of them)'.
segments.s165-g168: "همه کاخ" literally 'the whole palace' or 'all the palaces' (همه as totalizer); "زربفت‌ها" is a plural noun 'gold-brocades' (woven cloths of gold); "گسترید" is the past tense of 'to spread/extend' (they spread/laid out the brocades). The sequence describes the furnishing/decoration that followed the selection.
segments.s165-g601: کزین is an archaic contraction meaning "که از این / از این" (from this); it introduces origin. دو نژاده is a genitive/compound phrase meaning "two lineages/offspring" (نژاده = lineage), and یکی is the subject "one (of them)". نامور is a predicate adjective placed after the subject (poetic word order) meaning "renowned."
segments.s165-g602: برآرد is a third-person singular classical/poetic form meaning "will bring up / raise" (here: "raise up/lift"); in older Persian this form functions as a simple future/optative. به + خورشید تابنده سر is a prepositional phrase of direction/target: به (towards/to) + خورشید (sun) modified by the participial adjective تابنده (shining). سر is the direct object being "raised" ("head").
segments.s165-g603: بایران here is a contracted/poetic form corresponding to به ایران (in/over Iran); such prefixing is common in Ferdowsi. و is the coordinating conjunction "and." بود is the past copula "was," and شهریار is the predicate noun "king/sovereign." The overall order is locative/phrase + copula + predicate (poetic inversion).
segments.s165-g604: دو کشور is the subject phrase "the two countries/realms" (referring back to Iran and Turan). برآساید is a classical/poetic verb meaning "be at ease / be relieved / find peace" (third-person singular, optative/indicative sense in context). از کارزار is a prepositional phrase "from battle/warfare" marking the source from which they are relieved.
segments.s165-g605: وگر is the classical conditional "and if / but if." زین here is the poetic contraction of ز این ("from/about this"), introducing the object of the secret; نشان is "sign/token," راز is "secret," and دارد is the present/past form of داشتن "has/possesses." سپهر (the sky/heaven) appears as the agent; word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect (normally سپهر راز دارد).
segments.s165-g606: بیفزایدش is بیفزاید + ـش (object clitic) meaning "will add it / increase it" (third-person singular, optative/future nuance). هم means "also/too." باندیشه is a compound prepositional phrase "with thought/with intent" (بـ + اندیشه), and مهر here means "affection / loving kindness," so the phrase means "also adds it with affectionate intent."
segments.s165-g607: بخواهد is the third-person singular of خواستن used here to mean "he will desire / he wishes." بدن is "the body / bodily existence." بی‌گمان is an adverb "undoubtedly / surely," and بودنی is a substantive/adjectival form meaning "being / existence (of being)." Together they express "he desires bodily being, without doubt."
segments.s165-g608: نکاهد is a negative-form verb in classical Persian meaning "will not lessen / will not diminish" (the negative prefix نـ + کاهد/کاهش-root); the object is implicit. به here takes the instrumental/agentive sense "by/through," پرهیز means "abstinence / caution / forbearance," and افزودنی is an adjective/participle meaning "that which increases / additive." The line thus contrasts: he will not lessen (life) by abstinence, but will increase it (افزودنی) by forbearance/prudence.
segments.s165-g609: نگه کن is an imperative ('look' / 'take notice'); که introduces a content clause ('that'). این کار is the noun phrase 'this deed/act', and فرخ بود is adjective + past copula ('was fortunate'). The whole segment = 'Look/notice that this deed was fortunate.'
segments.s165-g610: ز بخت is a prepositional phrase 'by/through fate/fortune'. آنچ پرسند = 'that which they asked' (آنچ = 'what/that which', پرسند here functions as a past/relative 'asked'), and پاسخ بود = 'there was an answer'. Together: 'by fate, whatever they asked, there was an answer.'
segments.s165-g611: This is a poetic listing: ز تخم فریدون = 'from the seed/offspring of Fereydun'. The following وز should be read as a contraction of 'و ز' ('and from'), linking کیقباد ('Kay Qobad'). In other words: 'from the seed of Fereydun and from Kay Qobad'.
segments.s165-g612: فروزنده‌تر is the comparative 'more radiant/more splendid'. زین here functions in the comparative formula '... زِ این نباشد نژاد' meaning 'there is no race/lineage more ... than this'. نباشد is the negative copula/existential ('is not / there is not') and نژاد = 'lineage/race'. The clause = 'there is no lineage more radiant than this.'
segments.s165-g613: به پیران = 'to the elders' (prepositional indirect object); چنین گفت = 'spoke thus/said so'; پس شهریار places the subject 'then the king' (پس = 'then'). Word order is typical poetic: indirect object + verb + adverb + subject.
segments.s165-g614: که introduces the subordinate clause. رای تو = 'your counsel/plan' (subject). بر بد نیاید is an idiomatic negative expression meaning 'may it not come to/lead to bad (result)'; نیاید here has a hortative/optative sense ('may it not come' / 'should not come'). به کار is the complement 'in the affair/matter'.
segments.s165-g615: به فرمان و رای تو is a prepositional phrase 'at/by your command and counsel' (به governs the whole coordinated phrase). کردم سخن = 'I made speech' (کردم + object سخن) i.e. 'I spoke.' The verb is past first person.
segments.s165-g616: برو is imperative 'go'. هرچ = 'whatever/whichever', باید = modal 'must/ought to', and the clause 'هرچ باید ... بکن' means 'do whatever must be done'; به خوبی بکن is an adverbial phrase 'do (it) well'. The subject of بکن is implicit (do it).
segments.s165-g57: This clause states a change of state with the past verb 'شد' (became). Word order is Subject (خاک) + predicate adjective (مشکین) + verb (شد). 'همی' is a narrative/progressive particle used here to give a poetic sense of ongoing/resulting action. 'مشکین' is used adjectivally meaning 'musk‑scented' (not merely 'dark'), and 'از' introduces the source/means ('from/by') with the items that caused the scent: 'مشک و زر' (musk and gold).
segments.s165-g58: 'همی' again functions as a narrative/progressive particle. 'اسپ تازی' is a noun phrase with 'اسپ' (horse) modified by the adjective/ethnic 'تازی' (Arabian). The verb 'برآورد' here is a past action meaning 'raised/lifted up' (classical usage of برآورد as 'to raise' in poetry), and 'پر' (literally 'feather') names the thing raised — i.e., the horse's plume/tail or feathered trappings. The clause therefore describes the steed rearing or lifting its plumes.
segments.s165-g59: 'چو' introduces a temporal clause meaning 'when'. The sequence is name (سیاوش) + temporal marker + pronoun/anaphor (آن) + verb (دید). The phrase 'آب از دو چشم' is the direct object of the following verb (ببارید), i.e. 'water/tears from both eyes' — in Persian poetic usage 'آب' here means tears.
segments.s165-g60: 'ببارید' is a past tense verb meaning 'fell'/'came down in drops' (used of tears here). The coordinating 'و' links the two resulting actions. 'ز' (poetic variant of از) introduces cause/source with 'اندیشه' ('thought, memory'), and the inverted phrase 'آمد به خشم' literally 'anger came to him' is an idiomatic periphrasis meaning 'he became angry' — the verb آمد + به + noun expresses change into that state.
segments.s165-g61: 'که' here introduces a content/relative clause ('that/for'), and 'یاد آمدش' is an inverted construction: noun 'یاد' (memory) is the subject and 'آمدش' ('came to him' — آمد + enclitic ش = to him) is the verb phrase. 'بوم زابلستان' is a genitive noun phrase meaning 'the homeland of Zabulistan' (بوم = homeland); the whole clause means 'the memory of the homeland of Zabulistan came to him.'
segments.s165-g62: This short clause qualifies the memory: 'بیاراسته' (unadorned/despoiled) modifies the extent introduced by 'تا' meaning 'as far as' (extent marker), followed by the destination 'به کابلستان' ('to/unto Kabulistan'). Together they mean the memory reached him bare/desolate 'even up to Kabulistan.'
segments.s165-g63: 'همان' ('that very') specifies 'شهر' (city). 'ایرانش' is 'ایران' with the enclitic possessive 'ش' (= 'his/of him') attached; here it identifies the city as belonging in the Iranian domain connected to him. Again we have the inverted 'آمد به یاد' pattern: 'شهر ... آمد به یاد' = 'the city came to his memory.'
segments.s165-g64: 'همی' is the narrative/progressive particle. 'برکشید' is a past verb meaning 'raised/heaved up' (classical/poetic usage). The phrase 'از جگر' ('from the liver') is an idiomatic Persian expression meaning 'from the heart/inner being' and 'سرد باد' is a noun phrase 'cold wind.' The clause thus figuratively says a cold wind rose from his heart (i.e., a chill or sudden grief seized him).
segments.s165-g961: Two coordinated prepositional/causal phrases introduced by the poetic preposition ز (here meaning 'because of'/'on account of'). گفتار بدخواه is a genitive phrase, 'the speech/words of an ill‑wisher' (بدخواه is a noun meaning 'ill‑wisher'). The second phrase repeats ز + noun (بخت) + adjective (بد) to mean 'because of bad fortune.'
segments.s165-g962: چنین is an adverb meaning 'thus/so.' بی‌گنه ('innocent') describes the speaker. بر سرم is a prepositional phrase meaning 'upon me/over my head.' بد رسد is a short predicate where رسد (present/subjunctive sense 'to happen, befall') takes بد ('evil, ill') to express 'evil befalls (me)'; the subject is the speaker (implicit).
segments.s165-g963: ز here introduces the circumstance/condition (poetic usage). کشته شود is a passive verbal construction (شود + past participle) meaning 'be killed/shall be slain.' زندگانی is the noun 'life' and دژم is a postpositive adjective meaning 'sullen/grim,' so the phrase reads 'should (my) grim life be slain.'
segments.s165-g964: برآشوبد is a verb meaning 'become agitated' or 'be thrown into turmoil' (perfective/subjunctive register). ایران و توران is the coordinated subject 'Iran and Turan.' بهم after the subject means 'with one another' / 'together (in confusion),' marking reciprocal disorder between the two.
segments.s165-g965: پر از رنج گردد is a predicate meaning 'becomes full of suffering' (گردد = 'become'). سراسر زمین is the domain affected ('throughout the land' / 'the whole earth') and appears after the verb for poetic emphasis; together the clause says the land will be filled with hardship.
segments.s165-g966: دو کشور ('the two countries/realms') is the subject; شود is the copula/auxiliary 'will become.' پر ز شمشیر و کین is the predicate 'full of swords and vengeance/hatred' where ز marks the 'of' relation and کین is a noun 'vengeance, enmity.'
segments.s165-g967: بسی is a quantifier meaning 'many' / 'a great many' in classical Persian; it modifies the following coordinated list of colors (سرخ و زرد و سیاه و بنفش) describing the banners' hues. The list is a simple coordinate noun phrase enumerating colors.
segments.s165-g968: از ایران و توران is a prepositional source phrase 'from Iran and Turan.' ببینی is second‑person singular present/subjunctive of دیدن used narratively to mean 'you will see' (a classical/historical present with future force). درفش ('banner/standard') is the object seen; the construction literally says 'from Iran and Turan you will see (the) banners.'
segments.s165-g345: کمان را is the definite direct object ('the bow' + object marker را) placed before the verb, به زه is a prepositional phrase 'to the string' specifying where the bow was drawn, بر here functions as a prepositional/preverbal element ('upon/against' or 'toward') attached to the verbs, and بباز و فگند is a compound past action: an archaic/dialectal past form meaning 'drew/bent (the bow)' followed by و + فگند ('and loosed/threw'). Together they describe the single combined action of drawing and releasing the arrow.
segments.s165-g346: بیامد is a simple past (3rd sg) 'came/flew', بر is the preposition 'against/onto' indicating direction/impact, شهریار is the noun 'king' and بلند is a postpositive adjective 'lofty' modifying شهریار; the clause says the (loosed) missile came upon/at the lofty king.
segments.s165-g347: فرود آمد is a compound verbal expression in the past tense meaning 'fell/descended' (فرود = fall/alighting + آمد = came), و links the next clause, شاه is the subject of the following verb, برپای is an adverbial/phrase 'on (his) feet' and خاست is past 'rose/stood up' — together 'and the king rose to his feet.'
segments.s165-g348: برو آفرین here functions idiomatically as an exclamation ('Bravo!' or an uttered cry of praise) and is followed by ز ('from') introducing آفریننده ('the Creator') as the source, with خواست (past 'he wanted/asked') as the main verb meaning 'he desired/sought.' Overall: he uttered acclaim and sought (praise/blessing) from the Creator.
segments.s165-g349: وزان is an archaic fused prepositional phrase equivalent to و از آن ('and/from that') marking the point of departure, جایگه 'place' names the source location, سوی is the preposition 'toward', and کاخ بلند is the noun + postpositive adjective 'lofty palace' as the destination.
segments.s165-g350: برفتند is past (3rd pl) 'they went/proceeded', and the following phrase 'شادان دل' is a compound attributive/adverbial meaning 'with joyful hearts' (شادان modifying دل), while و ارجمند adds 'and honored/esteemed' describing their condition as they went.
segments.s165-g351: نشستند is a past plural verb 'they sat' (here 'took their seats'), خوان is the object 'the table/banquet', and می‌آراستند is an imperfective past-form of آراستن ('they were arranging/adorning/setting (the table/wine)'), so together: 'they sat at the feast and prepared/arranged the wine.'
segments.s165-g352: کسی ('someone/anyone') is followed by کاو, an archaic fused relative (καو = که او, 'who/that he'), so 'کسی کاو سزا بود' = 'anyone who was deserving', with سزا (desert/worthiness) + بود ('was') forming the relative predicate; بنشاستند (past 'they seated [him]') is the main verb meaning they caused such persons to sit down (they seated those who deserved it).
segments.s165-g889: بشد (beshd) is an archaic/past-form verb meaning 'it happened / he came' and here precedes the subject, a common epic word order (verb → subject). پور کاووس is a nominative phrase 'the son of Kavus' (پور = 'son'). و links this clause to what follows. آنجای (آنجا + ی) means 'there/at that place' and دید is the past tense 'he saw'.
segments.s165-g890: مر is the dative/benefactive clitic 'to/for me' (preposed here). آن را is the definite object 'that' with the object marker را. ز is the poetic preposition 'from', ایران is the place/name, همی is an archaic particle that emphasizes/frames the action (here functioning as an intensifier/old continuous marker), and برگزید is the past tense 'chose/selected' placed at the end as the main verb.
segments.s165-g891: تن خویش را is 'his own body' — خویش here is formed from خوی + the possessive ش (خوی → 'kin/one's own' + ش = 'his/her own'), and را marks the definite object. نامبردار کرد is a causative construction: نامبردار 'renowned' (adjective) + کرد 'made/did' → 'made (himself) renowned.'
segments.s165-g892: فزونی یکی literally orders the notion of 'an increase / one more' (فزونی = increase; یکی = one/more). نیز is 'also/too', دیوار is 'wall', and کرد is the past tense 'made/built' — overall: 'he also made/built one additional wall.' Word order places the noun phrase before the verb.
segments.s165-g893: The repeated preposition ز ('from/of') lists materials: ز سنگ و ز گچ = 'of stone and of plaster.' بود is the past copula 'was.' و چندی رخام = 'and some marble' (چندی = 'some/several', رخام = 'marble').
segments.s165-g894: وزان = 'and from that' (compositional link back to the preceding materials). جوهری کش is a compound: جوهری 'gemlike / jewelled' with کش as a formative element ('-strewn / -bearing'), i.e. 'studded with jewels.' ندانیم is the 1pl present/past-subjunctive form of دانستن with negative prefix ن- here meaning 'we do not know'; نام is 'name.' The phrase means 'jewelled (things) whose name we do not know.'
segments.s165-g895: دو صد رش is a numeral phrase 'two hundred [رش]' placed before the predicate. فزونست is a contracted classical form (فزون است) meaning 'is more / is in excess' — together conveying 'there are two hundred more above it.' بالای = 'above' and اوی is the enclitic pronoun 'it/he/she' referring back to the thing described.
segments.s165-g896: همان = 'that very / the same' (emphatic). سی و پنچ‌ست is an old-form numeric+copula construction (سی و پنچ + ست) meaning 'is thirty-five' (پنچ‌ست contains the copular -ست). پهنای اوی = 'its width' (پهنای = 'width of', اوی = possessive 'its'). The clause states 'its width is exactly thirty-five.'
segments.s165-g545: This is a nominal phrase joined by و: سپاه 'army' and the prepositional phrase در گنج من 'in my treasure/treasury'. پیش here functions adverbially ('before / at the disposal of / in the presence of'), and تست is a poetic/copular element meaning 'is yours / is before you' (a contraction of تو است in classical style). Word order places the things first and the locative/predicative element last.
segments.s165-g546: مرا here is topical/emphatic (as for me); the element م (from lemma #هست) functions as an existential/copular particle 'is/there is' in older Persian constructions. را is the object/topic marker attached to that topicalized form; سودمندی is the substantive 'usefulness', کم و بیش is a fixed adverbial pair 'less and more / more or less', and the final تست again supplies the copula ('is' / 'belongs to you') in poetic register. The verb phrase is delayed to the end, typical of Persian.
segments.s165-g547: کسی کاو is an older relative construction meaning 'anyone who' (کاو = که او). به introduces the locative/affected place 'in/under' (here 'in'), زندان و بند is a compound 'prison and fetters/straps', and منست is a poetic copula meaning 'is mine / belongs to me' (here functioning as the predicate). The relative clause places the person first and the state afterwards.
segments.s165-g548: گشادنش is the verbal noun/infinitive 'to open / to free him' with the enclitic object -ش (him). درد و گزند is a nominal phrase 'pain and harm', and منست again functions as the copula/predicate 'is to me' or 'affects me' in poetic diction. The structure states the consequence of releasing such a person.
segments.s165-g549: The repeated preposition ز (poetic ز for modern از) means 'from' or 'because of'. خشم و بند من is 'my anger and my bonds/ fetters', آزاد is the adjective 'free', and گشت is the past-tense/copular verb 'became'. The clause states a completed change of state: 'became free from...'.
segments.s165-g550: ز بهر تو = 'for your sake' (ز ... بهر is a classical sequence meaning 'for/on account of'); پیگار من is 'my quarrel / my battle'; باد is the optative particle 'may' and گشت 'became' yields a wish-like clause 'may my quarrel become (so) for you' — i.e. 'may my struggle be on your behalf'. The verb (گشت) appears after the optative particle, a typical classical ordering.
segments.s165-g551: ز بسیار و اندک uses ز as a poetic 'from/of' and pairs بسیار 'much/many' with اندک 'little/few' to set up a general condition ('whether much or little'). چه باید بخواه is a question: چه 'what', باید 'ought / must', and بخواه (the present/subjunctive/imperative stem of خواستن) 'you ask/seek' — here functioning with باید to mean 'what should you ask'. The verb is placed at the end, as usual.
segments.s165-g552: This is a list introduced by repeated ز 'from/of' naming possible things to request: تیغ 'sword', مهر 'favour/kindness' (not romantic love here), تخت 'throne', and کلاه 'hat' (in epic usage meaning helmet or crown). The repeated preposition is a classical poetic device to weigh each item equally.
segments.s165-g1193: بدانگه که = a temporal conjunction meaning 'when/at that time'; the clause یاد من آید به دست is literal 'the memory of me comes to (the) hand' — an idiom meaning 'you remember me' or 'my memory occurs to you'. Word order is Subject (یادِ من) + verb (آید) + prepositional phrase (به دست). آید is the simple present/indicative (classical style) meaning 'comes'.
segments.s165-g1194: چو introduces a subordinate temporal clause 'when'; خوردی here is the past/perfect second-person form of خوردن used idiomatically as 'befall/be seized by' (خوردن به چیزی). Thus چو خوردی به شادی = 'when you are overtaken by joy'. بباید نشست is an impersonal/modal construction: بباید (classical form of باید) 'one must/it is necessary' + نشست (bare stem used like an infinitive 'to sit/down') → 'one must sit (down)'.
segments.s165-g1195: یکی is an indefinite 'someone/a certain one' (subject). هدیه آرای is a compound-like phrase 'gift-adornings' or 'arraying of gifts' (هدیه + آرای(ی)), with بسیار ('many') as its quantifier. مر is an archaic/poetic form of مرا ('to me'), marking the indirect object (dative). The whole chunk means 'someone bedecked/treated me with many gifts.'
segments.s165-g1196: This is a list introduced by the preposition ز (poetic 'of/from'), enumerating the items: ز دینار (of dinars), وز اسب (and of horses — here وز functions as an archaic fused 'و ز' = 'and of'), و زرین کمر (and a golden belt). زرین modifies کمر ('golden girdle'). The prepositional z-phrases specify the contents of the 'many gifts.'
